description: Implemented Convex customer/coworker search latency improvements and verified gates plus experiment harness help

asi.hypothesis: Server-indexed customer search latency improves by avoiding no-op mirror writes, while coworker perceived latency improves through validated Convex cache plus frontend in-flight/TTL cache; docs and a guarded experiment harness make continued measurement repeatable.
asi.verification: bun run typecheck && bun run lint && bun run test && bun run search:experiment -- --help passed in run_experiment.
asi.caveat: Live destructive Convex latency experiment was not run; harness requires --destructive-ok and a disposable/dev deployment because it replaces customers, coworkerSearchCache, and feishuUserTokens.

description: Route warm coworker cache hits through a public Convex query before action fallback

asi.hypothesis: Warm coworker/contact search cache hits should avoid both Feishu calls and Convex action runtime by checking a session-validated public Convex query before falling back to the existing action.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed targeted coworker hook/server cache tests plus typecheck/lint with METRIC warm_cache_remote_calls=0; separate full suite passed 28 files / 210 tests and search:experiment help still works.
asi.anti_overfit: The test asserts behavior at the API boundary (no action call on warm query cache hit) rather than hardcoding benchmark internals; Feishu remains source of truth on cold/stale cache misses.
asi.caveat: Live destructive Convex p50/p95 experiment was not run because it requires --destructive-ok on a disposable/dev deployment.

description: Throttle on-search Customer Mirror full refresh kicks

asi.hypothesis: Repeated CustomerPicker opens can enqueue redundant full Customer Mirror refresh actions; throttling on-search kick to once per taskpane session every 15 minutes reduces Convex action load and Feishu records/search traffic without weakening query correctness because cache-miss backfill remains the per-query freshness path.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed new hook test plus typecheck/lint with METRIC mirror_refresh_actions=1 for two rapid triggerRefresh calls; separate full suite passed 29 files / 211 tests and search:experiment help still works.
asi.anti_overfit: The test checks the user-flow behavior (two rapid picker refresh triggers cause one kick action) rather than depending on implementation timing internals; no benchmark shortcuts or fake Feishu search results were added.
asi.caveat: This is a cost/latency-load reduction for repeated UI refresh triggers, not a live p50/p95 deployment measurement. Live destructive Convex experiment still requires --destructive-ok on a disposable/dev deployment.

description: Coalesce duplicate in-flight customer server-index searches

asi.hypothesis: Repeated identical CustomerPicker search calls can duplicate Convex query/action work while a previous server-index search is still in flight; coalescing by normalized query and owner filter reduces remote calls without caching stale customer data beyond the in-flight request.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed the customer server-index hook test plus typecheck/lint with METRIC customer_duplicate_remote_calls=1 for two concurrent equivalent searches; separate full suite passed 29 files / 212 tests and search:experiment help still works.
asi.anti_overfit: Only in-flight requests are coalesced; completed results are not persisted in the hook, so Feishu/Convex source-of-truth behavior for later searches remains unchanged.
asi.caveat: This measures duplicate-call suppression in a unit-level user-flow test rather than live p50/p95 on a deployment; the destructive Convex experiment still requires --destructive-ok on disposable/dev infrastructure.

description: Reduce Feishu page size for interactive customer cache-miss backfill

asi.hypothesis: Interactive customer cache misses should not fetch and upsert 500 Feishu rows when the picker displays at most 50; keeping full sync at page_size=500 while reducing searchAndCacheMiss to page_size=50 should reduce miss latency, payload, and Convex write fanout without harming normal mirror-hit correctness.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed customersMirror tests plus typecheck/lint with METRIC cache_miss_page_size=50; separate full suite passed 29 files / 213 tests and search:experiment help still works.
asi.anti_overfit: The change follows the product result limit and official Feishu page_size contract; it does not fake search results or skip Feishu on actual cache misses. Full sync still pages comprehensively at 500.
asi.caveat: A smaller first page can backfill fewer long-tail matches per miss; weekly full sync and later specific misses still populate the mirror. Live deployment p50/p95 remains to be measured on disposable/dev infrastructure.

name: Wire active Customer search hook to optimized implementations

description: Wire active customer search hook to focused optimized server-index implementation

asi.hypothesis: Previous customer server-index optimizations lived in the focused useCustomerSearchServerIndex hook but the active composite hook still carried an older inline implementation; routing useCustomerSearch through the focused hooks makes the duplicate-search coalescing and mirror-kick throttling affect the actual RequestIntakeScreen path.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed useCustomerSearch + useCustomerSearchServerIndex tests plus typecheck/lint with METRIC active_server_index_duplicate_calls=1.
asi.anti_overfit: This changes hook composition so production mode uses the already-tested focused implementation; it does not alter benchmark data, fake search results, or skip Feishu/Convex source-of-truth paths.
asi.caveat: Full suite was not used for this log because unrelated pre-existing uncommitted bitable/requestSync/schema changes caused failures; those unrelated changes were temporarily excluded from this commit and restored afterward.

description: Attempted CustomerPicker server-search debounce but verification was blocked by unrelated dirty backend changes

asi.rollback_reason: run_experiment failed during typecheck because unrelated pre-existing dirty backend changes in requestSync/bitable/schema were present and broke generated API/type assumptions, not because of the CustomerPicker debounce patch itself.
asi.next_action_hint: Temporarily isolate or stash unrelated backend changes, reapply only the CustomerPicker debounce patch, then rerun targeted test + typecheck/lint before logging keep.

description: CustomerPicker debounce rerun still blocked by dirty backend schema/emailRecord drift

asi.hypothesis: Debouncing CustomerPicker server fallback should collapse rapid keystrokes into one server search call.
asi.rollback_reason: run_experiment failed during typecheck because backend schema/emailRecord/requestSync state in the working tree/repo is inconsistent with generated API, outside the CustomerPicker debounce path.
asi.next_action_hint: Use a clean worktree or repair the unrelated backend schema/requestSync changes before running typecheck/lint-inclusive autoresearch for frontend debounce.

asi.next_action_hint: Isolate all unrelated bitable/requestSync/email sync work from autoresearch or complete that migration separately before using full-suite gates.

asi.hypothesis: A clean verification baseline is required before further search-latency iterations; removing the accidentally committed requestSync outbox drift restores typecheck/lint/full-test gates without touching search behavior.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed bun run typecheck, bun run lint, and bun run test with METRIC typecheck_errors=0; full suite passed 30 files / 214 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: This does not alter search code or benchmarks; it only restores consistency between requestSync, schema, and generated API so future latency experiments can be honestly verified.

description: CustomerPicker debounce hit max-lines lint guard

asi.hypothesis: Debouncing CustomerPicker server fallback should collapse rapid keystrokes into one server search call.
asi.rollback_reason: run_experiment passed the targeted test/typecheck portion but failed lint because CustomerPicker.tsx exceeded the repository max-lines rule after adding debounce test/code.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the debounce patch and either extract small helpers or update the file-level lint exception in line with existing CustomerPicker test conventions, then rerun the full gate.

description: Debounce CustomerPicker server fallback search calls

asi.hypothesis: Debouncing CustomerPicker server fallback should collapse rapid keystrokes into one server search call, reducing Convex query/action traffic and Feishu cache-miss pressure without changing search correctness.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed targeted CustomerPicker test, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC customer_picker_server_calls=1; an extra full-suite check also passed 30 files / 221 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The test simulates a real typing flow (n -> no -> novo) and asserts one server search with the final normalized query; local matches still short-circuit and Feishu/Convex source-of-truth paths are unchanged.
asi.caveat: This is a UI call reduction measurement, not a live p50/p95 deployment measurement. The previous crash in this segment reported metric 0 only because lint failed before a valid keep run, so it is not a real performance baseline.

description: Project Feishu fields for interactive customer cache-miss search

asi.hypothesis: Interactive customer cache-miss search currently asks Feishu for full Bitable rows; adding the same official records/search field_names projection used by full sync should reduce response payload and parsing time without changing the CustomerRecord projection or source-of-truth behavior.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed customersMirror tests, typecheck, lint, and full test suite with METRIC cache_miss_unbounded_field_fetch=0; search:experiment help still works.
asi.anti_overfit: The test asserts the real Feishu request shape includes the needed projected fields rather than mocking faster results. No search results are skipped or fabricated; cache misses still call Feishu records/search.
asi.caveat: This is a request-payload reduction verified at the API boundary, not a live deployment p50/p95 measurement.

description: Short-query CustomerPicker server guard blocked by ImportMetaEnv baseline type drift

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding server fallback for one-character CustomerPicker queries should reduce broad, low-quality cache-miss traffic while local matches still render immediately.
asi.rollback_reason: run_experiment passed the targeted CustomerPicker test but failed typecheck because ImportMetaEnv no longer included Vite DEV/MODE and CSS side-effect declarations; this is a baseline typing issue outside the short-query patch.
asi.next_action_hint: Repair vite-env declarations, then reapply /tmp/short-query-guard.patch and rerun the full gate.

description: Skip server fallback for one-character CustomerPicker queries

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding server fallback for one-character CustomerPicker queries should reduce broad, low-quality cache-miss traffic while local matches still render immediately and server search begins once the query is specific enough.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed targeted CustomerPicker test, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC short_query_server_calls=0.
asi.anti_overfit: The test simulates a real one-character typing flow and verifies no server fallback call; it does not fake search results or skip server search for meaningful two-character-plus queries.
asi.caveat: This is a UI call guard measurement, not live p50/p95 deployment data.

description: Guard server-index customer search against one-character queries

asi.hypothesis: The CustomerPicker now suppresses one-character server fallback, but the server-index search adapter could still be called directly by other UI paths or future refactors; adding the same minimum-query guard at the adapter boundary prevents broad Convex mirror queries and cache-miss Feishu calls without changing meaningful two-character-plus searches.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed useCustomerSearchServerIndex tests, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC server_index_short_query_remote_calls=0; full suite passed 30 files / 223 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The test exercises the public hook API with a real one-character query and asserts no Convex query or cache-miss action is called; it does not fake results and leaves all two-character-plus search paths unchanged.
asi.caveat: This is a boundary call-reduction guard, not live p50/p95 deployment data.

description: Guard customer cache-miss action against one-character Feishu searches

asi.hypothesis: The UI and server-index hook now suppress one-character searches, but the public cache-miss action could still be invoked directly or by future code paths; guarding the action itself prevents broad low-quality Feishu records/search calls and Convex mirror writes for one-character queries without changing meaningful two-character-plus searches.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed customersMirror tests, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC cache_miss_short_query_feishu_calls=0; full suite passed 30 files / 224 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The test calls the cache-miss action handler with a real one-character query and asserts no Feishu call or Convex mutation occurs; it leaves actual two-character-plus cache misses untouched and does not fake search results.
asi.caveat: This is a defensive API-boundary call reduction, not live deployment p50/p95 data.

description: Suppress repeated empty customer live cache-miss actions

asi.hypothesis: Repeated searches for a query that returned no live Customer results can otherwise hit the Convex action and Feishu records/search every time because there is nothing to backfill into the mirror. Briefly remembering empty live misses after first rechecking the Convex mirror should reduce repeated action/Feishu work without hiding newly mirrored data.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed useCustomerSearchServerIndex tests, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC empty_miss_live_actions=1; full suite passed 30 files / 225 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The adapter still performs the Convex mirror query before suppressing the live action, so data warmed by full sync or another user can appear immediately. Only empty live results are remembered for a short 30s TTL; positive results clear the miss cache and source-of-truth paths remain unchanged.
asi.caveat: This reduces repeat empty-miss action/Feishu load in a hook-level flow; live p50/p95 remains deferred until disposable/dev deployment measurement.

description: Skip remote coworker search for one-character queries

asi.hypothesis: Coworker Search Users calls for one-character queries are broad and low-value; adding the same two-character minimum at the coworker hook boundary should avoid Convex query/action work and Feishu round trips while keeping meaningful two-character-plus searches unchanged.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed useCoworkerSearch tests, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC coworker_short_query_remote_calls=0; full suite passed 30 files / 226 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The test exercises the public hook with a real one-character query and asserts neither the warm Convex cache query nor Feishu action fallback is called. No results are fabricated; preview/test fixtures remain local and normal two-character-plus Search Users calls are unchanged.
asi.caveat: This is a UI/hook boundary call-reduction metric, not live p50/p95 Feishu deployment data.

description: Guard coworker search action against one-character queries

asi.hypothesis: The coworker hook now suppresses one-character remote searches, but the public Convex action could still be invoked directly or by future UI paths; enforcing the same two-character minimum in the action avoids token resolution, cache query/mutation work, and Feishu Search Users calls for broad low-value one-character input while preserving two-character-plus searches.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed convex/feishu/coworkers.test.ts, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC coworker_action_short_query_remote_calls=0; full suite passed 30 files / 227 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The test calls the actual action handler with a real one-character query and asserts no token resolution, cache query/mutation, or Feishu call occurs. It does not fabricate coworkers; normal two-character-plus Search Users paths and cache behavior are unchanged.
asi.caveat: This is a defensive API-boundary call-reduction measurement, not live Feishu p50/p95 deployment data.

description: Guard coworker warm-cache query against one-character reads

asi.hypothesis: The coworker hook and action now suppress one-character searches, but the public warm-cache query could still be invoked directly or by future code paths; returning before session/cache reads for one-character input avoids unnecessary Convex DB work while preserving all two-character-plus warm-cache behavior.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed convex/feishu/coworkers.test.ts, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC coworker_cached_short_query_db_reads=0; full suite passed 30 files / 228 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The test calls the actual public query handler with a real one-character query and spies on the fake DB to assert no session or cache read occurs. It returns no synthetic coworkers and leaves authenticated two-character-plus cache lookup behavior unchanged.
asi.caveat: This is a defensive query-boundary DB-read reduction, not live p50/p95 deployment data.

name: Customer mirror public search short-query guard

description: Guard customer mirror public search against one-character index reads

asi.hypothesis: The UI, server-index hook, and cache-miss action already suppress one-character customer searches, but the public mirror search query could still be called directly or by future code paths; applying the same two-character boundary before the search-index query avoids broad low-value Convex search index reads while preserving mirroredAt and two-character-plus search behavior.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed convex/feishu/customersMirror.test.ts, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC customer_mirror_short_query_index_reads=0; full suite passed 30 files / 229 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The test calls the actual public query handler with a real one-character query and asserts the customers search-index path is not reached. It does not fabricate customers; normal two-character-plus mirror search and cache-miss source-of-truth paths are unchanged.
asi.caveat: This is a defensive query-boundary search-index-read reduction, not live p50/p95 deployment data.

description: Skip CoworkerPicker debounce for one-character production queries

asi.hypothesis: After adding short-query guards in the coworker hook and Convex endpoints, CoworkerPicker still scheduled a debounce timer for one-character production input before the hook returned empty results. Clearing immediately and waiting until two characters avoids timer/hook overhead and stale result display while preserving preview fixtures and normal Feishu Search Users behavior.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed CoworkerPicker tests, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC coworker_picker_short_query_hook_calls=0; full suite passed 31 files / 231 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The test exercises the real CoworkerPicker UI with a one-character query and verifies no hook call or dropdown opens; it also verifies two-character queries still debounce and call the hook. No coworkers are fabricated and the Feishu source-of-truth path is unchanged.
asi.caveat: This is a UI timer/hook-call reduction and stale-result prevention, not live Feishu p50/p95 deployment data.

description: Cap CustomerPicker local filtering at displayed result count

asi.hypothesis: In preload mode, broad CustomerPicker queries can locally scan the entire Customer Directory even though the dropdown only renders the first eight options. Stopping local filtering once the visible result count is full should reduce taskpane CPU work for broad matches without changing visible results or server source-of-truth behavior.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed customerSearchHelpers + CustomerPicker tests, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC local_filter_records_scanned_for_broad_match=8; full suite passed 32 files / 232 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The helper test uses real matching CustomerRecord objects with observable name reads to verify early exit after the display cap. The no-match path still scans the directory to avoid hiding create-customer/server-fallback behavior, and server-index/Feishu source-of-truth paths are unchanged.
asi.caveat: This measures local CPU work for broad local matches, not live browser p95 typing latency.

description: Keep CustomerPicker closed for one-character no-match queries

asi.hypothesis: After short-query server guards, CustomerPicker still opened an empty/create dropdown for one-character queries with no local matches. Keeping the dropdown closed until either local matches exist or the query is specific enough avoids unnecessary DOM/listbox work and prevents low-quality one-character create affordances while preserving immediate local matches.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed CustomerPicker tests, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC customer_picker_short_query_empty_dropdowns=0; full suite passed 32 files / 233 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The tests exercise real CustomerPicker UI for both sides of the boundary: one-character no-match stays closed/no server call, while a one-character local match still renders immediately. No search results are fabricated and two-character-plus server/create behavior remains unchanged.
asi.caveat: This is a UI DOM/affordance reduction, not live browser p95 typing latency data.

name: CustomerPicker short-query duplicate local scan removal

description: Skip duplicate local scan in CustomerPicker short-query server guard

asi.hypothesis: For one-character CustomerPicker input, the server-search guard does not need to run its own local filter because rendering already computes local matches. Returning before the server-decision local filter should halve local scan work for one-character no-match queries while preserving immediate local matches and all two-character-plus server fallback behavior.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed CustomerPicker tests, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC short_query_customer_local_name_reads=100; full suite passed 32 files / 234 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The test exercises the real CustomerPicker UI with 100 observable Customer names and verifies exactly one render-time local scan for a one-character no-match query. A companion test still verifies one-character local matches render, and server/Feishu behavior remains gated at two characters.
asi.caveat: This measures local CPU/name-read work in a unit-level UI flow, not live browser p95 typing latency.

description: Skip customer auto-match remote work for invalid email domains

asi.hypothesis: Customer auto-match only has a useful mirror match/refresh path when the client email has a valid domain. Guarding invalid or incomplete emails before matchEmail/triggerRefresh avoids unnecessary Convex mirror calls and mirror kicks while preserving valid-domain auto-match behavior.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed useCustomerAutoMatch tests, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC invalid_email_customer_remote_triggers=0; full suite passed 33 files / 237 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The tests exercise the real hook for no-domain, incomplete-domain, and valid-domain inputs. They assert invalid inputs do no remote trigger work while valid domains still call matchEmail and triggerRefresh; no Customer results are fabricated and source-of-truth paths remain unchanged.
asi.caveat: This is a remote-trigger suppression metric at hook level, not live Convex p50/p95 deployment data.

description: Coalesce customer auto-match remote work by email domain

asi.hypothesis: Customer auto-match is domain-based, so editing only the email local part should not repeat matchEmail or triggerRefresh for the same domain. Remembering the attempted domain in the auto-match hook should reduce redundant Convex mirror match/refresh triggers while still trying again when the domain changes.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed useCustomerAutoMatch tests, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC same_domain_customer_match_email_calls=1; full suite passed 33 files / 238 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The test exercises the real hook by rerendering from buyer@example.com to accounts@example.com and verifies one remote match/refresh for the shared domain. The valid-domain test still proves initial remote work runs, and invalid-domain guards remain intact; no Customer results are fabricated.
asi.caveat: This is a hook-level remote-trigger coalescing metric, not live Convex p50/p95 deployment data.

name: Customer auto-match same-domain local scan coalescing

description: Customer auto-match local scan coalescing test expected dispatch stability too strictly

asi.hypothesis: Customer auto-match local directory matching is domain-based, so changing only the email local part should not rescan the directory for the same domain.
asi.rollback_reason: run_experiment failed in the new targeted test because the assertion also expected dispatch to be called only once; the current hook still dispatches during render on rerender even though the domain scan was successfully not repeated. The failure is in the overly broad test assertion, not the local-scan optimization itself.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply /tmp/same-domain-local-scan.patch, narrow the test to the intended metric (domain reads stay at 100), and rerun the full gate.

description: Customer auto-match local scan coalescing hit unused test variable

asi.hypothesis: Customer auto-match local directory matching is domain-based, so changing only the email local part should not rescan the directory for the same domain.
asi.rollback_reason: The targeted test passed, but typecheck failed because the narrowed test left an unused dispatch binding. The implementation and intended metric were not invalidated.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply /tmp/same-domain-local-scan-v2.patch, remove the unused dispatch binding, and rerun full verification.

description: Coalesce customer auto-match local directory scans by email domain

asi.hypothesis: Customer auto-match local directory matching is domain-based, so editing only the email local part should not rescan the directory for the same domain. Keying local auto-match by derived domain should reduce repeated directory scans while still rematching when the domain changes.
asi.verification: run_experiment passed useCustomerAutoMatch tests, typecheck, lint, full test suite, and search:experiment help with METRIC same_domain_customer_local_domain_reads=100; full suite passed 33 files / 239 tests.
asi.anti_overfit: The test uses 100 Customer records with observable domain reads and rerenders from buyer@example.com to accounts@example.com; reads stay at one full scan for the shared domain. No Customer results are fabricated, and domain changes still alter the dependency key and rematch normally.
asi.caveat: The two prior crash logs in this metric segment recorded metric 0 before valid verification and are not real performance baselines; this keep represents the first verified run for the intended metric.
